prompt: Create a photorealistic dusk street photograph of a narrow Harajuku back alley where an entire concrete wall has been turned into a giant pastel KAWAII graffiti mural for Nozomu. The mural shows a huge anime-style girl painted on the wall, with silvery blonde wavy twin-tail hair, big sparkling blue eyes, flushed cheeks, a tiny surprised mouth, blue bows, heart hair clips, frilly lace accessories, and a light blue pastel dress covered with small hearts, stars, bows, and plush-toy motifs. Make the wall texture visibly real: cinderblock seams, rough paint, overspray, drips, weathering, and graffiti strokes integrated into the illustration, as if it was actually spray-painted at large scale rather than digitally pasted. Surround the character with bubbly Harajuku pop graffiti: oversized blue and pink heart balloons, small hearts, stars, clouds, cute bear-like mascot faces, a pink round mascot near the bottom right, and layered bubble outlines in cyan, lavender, hot pink, and cream. Include exactly 5 prominent text elements on the mural: a cloud at upper left reading "HARAJUKU DREAM", a large rounded Japanese name logo reading "のぞむ", a big Roman-letter name below reading "NOZOMU", a small speech-bubble label reading "TAKESHITA POP", and a lower-left bubble label reading "KAWAII STREET". The setting is a real urban alley at blue hour with wet-looking asphalt, cables and building facades receding into the distance, warm shop lights at the far right, and exactly 5 rectangular wall lamps casting warm yellow light across the mural. At the base of the mural, place exactly 6 spray paint cans clustered on the curb to imply the work was freshly painted. Use a wide-angle street-photo perspective from close to the wall, looking diagonally down the alley, with the mural dominating the frame, vibrant pastel neon colors, realistic shadows, ambient evening blue light, and a believable Instagram-worthy street art photo spot in Harajuku. No watermark, no extra text beyond the 5 specified mural text elements.
